<!--
Fiducial Mesh PR template. Delete sections that don't apply.
The bar: spec-adherent, conformance-clean, reviewed. Drift from the
Specification is a finding, not a feature.
-->

## What & why

<!-- One paragraph: what this changes and the problem it solves. -->

## Scope

- [ ] **Spec** (`FIDUCIAL-MESH-SPEC-001`) — normative requirement change
- [ ] **Handbook** (`FIDUCIAL-MESH-HDBK-001`) — narrative / rationale
- [ ] **Implementation** (a pillar / MCC / PCS plugin)
- [ ] **Tooling / CI / infra**
- [ ] **Docs only**

## Spec adherence

<!-- Cite the requirements this touches by ID, e.g. [FM-IAM-0004]. Every
cite is verified against the spec text (grep), not recalled. If this
introduces or changes a normative requirement, say so explicitly and bump
the version. -->

- Requirements touched: `[FM-…]`
- [ ] Consistent with the current `SPEC-001` (no undocumented drift)
- [ ] If normative: version bumped + Revision History / changelog updated
- [ ] Cross-document coherence held (SPEC ⇄ HDBK ⇄ any companion artifact)

## Conformance & tests

- [ ] Conformance/validation passes for the affected surface
- [ ] Tests added or updated; existing tests green
- [ ] No credentials, tokens, keys, or routable internal addresses introduced

## Review

- [ ] Independent review obtained (author is not the sole reviewer)
- [ ] Adversarial / first-principles concerns surfaced and addressed

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Where to look first; any deliberate deferrals (with the reason); any
open question you want pressure on. -->
